candidate_llm_application: Phalanx-overlap MAS ring defense: agents in ring, each runs safety-checker over self AND left-neighbor; attack requires compromising contiguous neighbors (contiguous-range agreement); distinct from majority/BFT threshold voting.

results.title: Hoplite — Wikipedia
results.rationale: Pure source-domain (ancient Greek hoplite warfare). Contains nothing about LLM/MAS agent ring defense, neighbor-overlap safety checks, or contiguous-range agreement.

results.title: Phalanx — Wikipedia
results.rationale: Pure source-domain (phalanx formation, othismos). No LLM-side application content.

results.title: The Greek Phalanx — World History
results.rationale: Pure source-domain article on Greek phalanx. No agent-side overlap-defense content.

results.title: AMAS: Adaptive Communication Topology for LLM-MAS (2025/10)
results.rationale: AMAS designs task-adaptive topologies for LLM-MAS but does not propose a ring with contiguous-range neighbor-overlap safety check. Only 'topology' is a generic shared word; no overlap/contiguous/neighbor-shield specific match.

results.title: Can AI Agents Agree? (2026/3)
results.rationale: BFT-style honest-agent consensus is threshold-based not contiguous-range. Shares 'agreement' generically but not contiguous-range or neighbor-overlap. Below 0.7 on all three axes.

results.title: Weighted BFT Consensus Multi-LLM Network (2025/5)
results.rationale: WBFT weighted-vote consensus; threshold/quorum not contiguous-range neighbor overlap. Functional mechanism differs (weight on votes vs spatial neighbor concurrence). Below threshold on all axes.

results.title: Topological Structure Learning Priority for LLM-MAS (2025/5)
results.rationale: Advocates topology-aware MAS design but does not specify contiguous-range overlap defense. Only generic 'topology' overlaps. Below threshold on all axes.

results.title: G-Safeguard: Topology-Guided MAS Security (2025/2)
results.rationale: G-Safeguard uses GNN-based topology anomaly detection plus intervention. Defense mechanism differs from hard architectural contiguous-range neighbor-overlap requirement. Functional similarity is in 'topology-based MAS defense' generally but not in the specific overlap-ring contiguous-concurrence mechanism. Below 0.7 on all axes.

summary.notes: All 8 results fall below the OR-threshold. Source-domain results (1-3) are pure history articles. LLM-side results (4-8) all describe different agent-coordination mechanisms (adaptive topology, BFT/WBFT consensus, GNN-anomaly detection, topology-aware design) — none implement the specific contiguous-range neighbor-overlap shield-wall defense the candidate proposes.
